codeReview.summary: Feature adds 8 comprehensive tests for OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions. Tests cover gen_ai module keys, provider conventions, pricing for Anthropic/OpenAI models, cost calculation, latency tracking, model attributes, and token-to-cost calculation. All tests pass. Implementation correctly validates the existing gen_ai module, LlmProvider enum, TokenPricing struct, and LatencyTracker.

issues.description: Test test_otel_genai_latency_tracker_precision has unusual #[allow(unused_comparisons)] attribute with comment explaining that u64 >= 0 is always true. This is technically correct (u64 is always >= 0) but the attribute usage is unconventional. The test still validates the correct behavior (elapsed2 >= 10 for 10ms sleep). Justified given the explanation in comments.

The test test_otel_genai_latency_tracker_precision has an assertion `elapsed >= 0` which is always true for u64. The comment explains this is to document intent, which is reasonable, but the assertion provides no actual test value. However, the subsequent assertion `elapsed2 >= 10` does provide real validation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Rust skill procedure was followed correctly. Worker used proper cargo commands (cargo build --workspace, cargo test -p swell-core -- otel_genai, cargo clippy -p swell-core). The skill suggests 'cargo test -p <package>' for unit tests, and worker correctly scoped to swell-core package.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: cargo build --workspace, cargo test -p swell-core -- otel_genai, cargo clippy -p swell-core -- -D warnings

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker did not use any services not already in services.yaml. All commands (cargo build, cargo test, cargo clippy) are properly documented in services.yaml under the 'commands' section.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: services.yaml defines: build, test, lint, workspace-build commands

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered and correctly used the gen_ai module keys and semantic conventions. The existing implementation already had gen_ai::OPERATION_NAME, gen_ai::PROVIDER_NAME, etc. The worker correctly identified that tests needed to be added to verify the 'otel_genai' feature filter. No online documentation needed - OpenTelemetry semantic conventions are properly documented in code comments.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: crates/swell-core/src/opentelemetry.rs lines 14-47 define gen_ai module with semantic convention keys

summary: PASS - Feature adds 8 comprehensive tests validating OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions including gen_ai.* attribute keys, provider conventions (Anthropic, OpenAI, Mock), model pricing accuracy, cost calculation, latency tracking, and token-to-cost conversion. All tests pass. Implementation correctly follows Rust conventions and skill procedures. No blocking issues found.
